rulings.Case Title: NIZAR FAKHOURI et al VS. REGENTS OF UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, February 3, 2015, Line 3, DEFENDANT THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication. The motion for summary judgment/summary adjudication is denied. Defendant failed to maintain its burden of production. To prevail on this motion, Defendant is obliged to show that Plaintiff's claims "cannot be established." (CCP 437c(p)(2)). At Defendant's Undisputed Material Facts 4 and 6, Plaintiff and Mr. Yasin clearly identify Defendant's van as the vehicle involved in the accident. The trier of fact will determine whether defendant's van caused the injury or whether plaintiff and his witness misidentified the van. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order in compliance with CCP 437c(g) with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: SHOPOFF CAVALLO & KIRSH LLP VS. PETER PAPPAS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Quashing Deposition Subpoena Served On Mark Basil Pappas, Attorney For Defendant And Cross-Complainants And Or For Protective Order; Request For Reasonable Expense In Making This Motion
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, February 3, 2015, Line 3, DEFENDANTs PETER PAPPAS, SOPHIE PAPPAS' Motion For Order Quashing Deposition Subpoena Served On Mark Basil Pappas, Attorney For Defendant And Cross-Complainants And Or For Protective Order; Request For Reasonable Expense In Making This Motion. Pro Tem Judge Kathy Gallo, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion to quash or in the alternative for protective order is denied on procedural grounds. The moving papers were untimely filed as they were filed 12 court days before the hearing not 16 court days before the hearing as required by CCP §1005(b). Moving party also failed to file a separate statement of items in dispute as is required by CRC 3.145(a)(5). The notice also fails to indicate the time or which department the hearing is to be held in violation of CCP §1010, CRC 3.1110(b) and San Francisco Superior Court Local Rules Of Court, Rule 10. The motion to quash and in the alternative for protective order is also denied on its merits. Attorney Mark Basil Pappas not only may he have personal knowledge of non-privileged information; he also may have in his possession non-privileged documents regarding the underlying cases, which are the subject of this lawsuit. If any question at deposition or document falls within any privilege Attorney Mark Basil Pappas still can assert his objections. Attorney Mark Basil Pappas must appear for his deposition with documents responsive to the request at the law offices of Hinshaw & Culbertson on a mutually agreed upon date and time for his deposition. If the parties cannot agree on date and time, then the deposition shall go forward on Friday, March 6, 2015 at 9:30 a.m. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to klgallo@discoveryreferee.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: JOY B VS. ANDREW S.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Reponses To Defendant Andrew Sauses Request For Production Of Documents By Plaintiff (Set Two) And For Sanctions_1041722
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, February 3, 2015, Line 10, DEFENDANT ANDREW SAUSE's Motion To Compel Further Reponses To Defendant Andrew Sause's Request For Production Of Documents By Plaintiff (Set Two) And For Sanctions. The Motion is GRANTED. The Motion is timely. (See Stimpert Declaration, Exhibit F, p. 3, par. 3). Plaintiff alleges extreme back pain in the Complaint. Plaintiff's counsel intimates that plaintiff suffered physical injuries to her spine from a car accident. (See Stimpert Declaration, Exhibit B, p. 3, par. 6). Plaintiff shall provide an amended, verified, code-compliant response to RFP 22, along with responsive documents, within two weeks of notice of entry of this order. The request for sanctions is GRANTED in the amount of $250. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. rulings.Case Title: JOY B VS. ANDREW S.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Make Defendants Name Anonymous
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, February 3, 2015, Line 10, DEFENDANT ANDREW SAUSE's Motion To Make Defendants Name Anonymous. The motion is denied. Courts do not treat the alleged victim of a sexual tort and the alleged perpetrator the same when it comes to anonymity. "If, as J.C. suggests, these mere accusations are tantamount to an irreparable injury sufficient to outweigh the public's interest in open proceedings, then he is really asking us to effectively grant all defendants accused of sexual abuse in civil cases the right to defend anonymously, a result which hardly comports with a philosophy granting anonymity only in rare circumstances." See T.S.R. v. J.C. (1996) 288 N.J. Super. 48, 58. The public has an interest in Defendant's identity if in fact he poses a danger to the community. Defendant's remedy is to win the case and be exonerated. Lastly, it is unclear why defendant is bringing this motion now. The case has been pending for almost a year and numerous pleadings have been filed and scanned. To meaningfully effectuate an order, numerous documents must be withdrawn and refiled, which would place an insurmountable burden on the court. Plaintiff, on the other hand, consistently sought to proceed anonymously. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: YIU LUN WONG VS. HON WEI CHAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reasonable Attorneys' Fees And Costs Pursuant To Labor Code Sec 98.2(C)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, February 3, 2015, Line 12, RESPONDENT HON CHAN's Motion For Reasonable Attorneys' Fees And Costs Pursuant To Labor Code Sec 98.2(C). Granted. Defendant Yiu Lun Wong shall pay Plaintiff Hon Wei Chan reasonable attorney's fees in the amount of $24,913.50 and costs in the amount of $1,602.00 within 30 days of entry of this order, pursuant to Lab. Code sec. 98.2(c). The plain language of Lab. Code sec. 98.2(c) provides that an employee is the prevailing party if the court amounts an award greater than zero. Plaintiff recovered $6,074.53 upon Defendant dismissing the appeal, and therefore, Plaintiff is entitled to recover fees and costs. Equitable principles support this result. Because Defendant filed a timely notice of appeal, Plaintiff was obligated to expend attorney's fees and costs through the date of trial. It would be inequitable to require Plaintiff to bear the burden of these costs. Further, Arias v. Kardoulias (2012) 207 Cal.App.4th 1429, the only case cited by Defendant in opposition, is inapposite. There, the trial court never obtained jurisdiction over the matter because the appellant did not timely file a notice of appeal. (Id. at 1438.) The court in Arias expressly limited its holding, stating that it "express[es] no opinion on the case in which a timely appeal from a commissioner's decision is dismissed on other grounds." (Id. at 1439, fn. 5.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: S. LOUIS MARTIN VS. GOOGLE, INC
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Vacate Strike Order Of 13 November 2014 In Case 539972, S. Louis Martin V Google, Inc.
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, February 3, 2015, Line 13, PLAINTIFF S. MARTIN's Motion To Vacate Strike Order Of 13 November 2014 In Case 539972, S. Louis Martin V Google, Inc. The motion is denied. First, plaintiff fails to support the motion with any competent evidence. Second, the motion violates CCP 1008. Third, there is no basis for relief under CCP 473. The court denies plaintiff's continuance request. Plaintiff set the hearing date and there is no basis for a continuance.
